X-H2S COMPANION

Tutorial · Presets · SOS · Manual · Exercises

More

Cheat sheet

Shutter button: half-press to focus · press all the way for the shot.
Lost in the menu? Press DISP/BACK a few times.
Presets save changes automatically (Auto Update ON). The Q menu shows the current state; the intended state lives on the “My Setup” page. Protected mode: MENU → IQ → AUTO UPDATE CUSTOM SETTING → OFF
Exposure triangle: aperture (small number = blurry background) · shutter speed (fast = freezes motion) · ISO (higher = brighter but grainier).
Shot too bright/dark: check exposure correction (±) first.
Mode A = you choose the aperture, the camera handles the rest. The best first step away from the presets.

Key menu paths

QQuick menu: the 16 most important settings at a glance
AUTO UPDATEMENU → IQ → AUTO UPDATE CUSTOM SETTING — ON: presets remember changes · OFF: presets snap back
FORMATMENU → wrench icon → USER SETTING → FORMAT (erases everything!)
FILM SIMULATIONMENU → IQ (camera 1) → FILM SIMULATION — or in the Q menu
SUBJECT DETECTIONMENU → AF/MF → SUBJECT DETECTION SETTING (animal, bird, …)
Fn BUTTONSLong-press DISP/BACK — or MENU → wrench icon → BUTTON/DIAL SETTING
JPEG/HEIFMENU → IQ → JPEG/HEIF SETTING — recommendation: HEIF (better quality, smaller file)
RAW CONVERSIONPlayback → Q button (with a RAW image shown) or MENU → RAW CONVERSION — develop RAW files right in the camera (p. 220)
CROPPlayback → MENU → CROP — reframe a shot, also works on copies you've already created (p. 225)
CARD BACKUPMENU → wrench icon → DATA STORAGE SETTING — photos written to both cards (p. 281)
GEOTAGGINGMENU → Network/USB → GEOTAGGING → ON (XApp paired)
TIME DIFFERENCEMENU → wrench icon → USER SETTING → TIME DIFFERENCE

Image formats & RAW development

What's what?

RAW — the digital negative: all the sensor data, huge headroom for editing, but it has to be “developed” (in the camera, see below, or on a computer). For images that matter and tricky light.

JPEG — the classic finished image (8-bit). Compatible everywhere, but technically dated.

HEIF — the modern JPEG successor (10-bit color depth): noticeably more quality headroom at smaller file sizes. iPhone, Mac and current Windows display HEIF natively.

How to use which? Everyday: HEIF. Important subjects / tricky light: RAW + HEIF (both get saved). JPEG only if an old device or web service won't accept HEIF — or generate one afterwards via RAW conversion.

Where to set this?
Format: MENU → IQ → JPEG/HEIF SETTING
RAW too: MENU → IQ → IMAGE QUALITY → e.g. RAW+
RAW compression: MENU → IQ → RAW RECORDING (“lossless compressed” is a good default)

Developing RAW in the camera — no computer needed, right on the trip:

  1. Show the image in playback (RAW icon) → press Q (the quickest way) or MENU/OK → RAW CONVERSION
  2. Set FILE FORMAT to HEIF first — this decides the copy's output format.
  3. Adjust any other settings you need (focus lever: highlight → right opens options → choose → MENU/OK confirms; full list of settings below).
  4. The Q button shows a preview of the copy.
  5. MENU/OK saves the copy as a new file — the original RAW stays unchanged.

Important: always do step 2 first. If FILE FORMAT isn't changed, you accidentally get an old-style JPEG instead of a high-quality HEIF — regardless of what else you change.

This is also how a C1 black & white RAW can become a color image after the fact: switch FILM SIMULATION to PROVIA or similar in step 3.

Only original RAW files can be converted. A HEIF or JPEG copy you already created isn't a RAW file itself, so it won't show up in RAW conversion as a source image.

Reframing a shot (cropping)

RAW conversion itself has no crop option. For reframing, the playback menu has its own CROP function — it works on RAW files just as well as on HEIF/JPEG copies you've already created.

To end up with a shot that's both edited and cropped:

  1. Create the desired edit via RAW conversion (see above).
  2. Navigate to the newly created copy in playback.
  3. Apply the CROP function to that copy.
Every RAW-conversion setting explained
REFLECT SHOOTING CONDITIONSCreates a copy using exactly the settings from when you shot it — the fast way back to the original state.
FILE FORMATAlways set this to HEIF first. The copy's output format — HEIF gives better quality (10-bit) at a smaller file size, JPEG is only for old devices/web services.
IMAGE SIZEThe copy's resolution (L/M/S) — smaller for quick sharing on the road, L for keeping/printing.
IMAGE QUALITYCompression level (Fine/Normal) — pick Fine for images that matter.
PUSH/PULL PROCESSINGPull exposure brighter/darker after the fact. Work in small steps — big jumps produce visible noise.
DYNAMIC RANGERecovers more detail in bright areas of the image — helpful in high-contrast light (sky, beach).
D RANGE PRIORITYReduces lost detail in highlights and shadows at the same time — the stronger option for harsh contrast.
FILM SIMULATIONChange the image look after the fact — e.g. turn a C1 black & white RAW into a color image with PROVIA.
MONOCHROMATIC COLORA color tint for black & white images (ACROS/MONOCHROME only) — e.g. a slightly warm or cool cast.
GRAIN EFFECTAdds film grain after the fact — an analog look.
COLOR CHROME EFFECTMore detail in strongly saturated reds, yellows and greens.
COLOR CHROME FX BLUEThe same, for blue tones — e.g. sky and water.
WHITE BALANCECorrect a color cast if it was set wrong while shooting.
WHITE BALANCE SHIFTFine-tune white balance (toward amber/blue or green/magenta).
TONE CURVEAdjust contrast in highlights and shadows separately.
COLORIncrease or decrease color saturation.
SHARPNESSSharpen edges, or render them softer.
HIGH ISO NRReduces image noise — useful for night/indoor shots at high ISO.
CLARITYBoosts fine contrast without harsh re-sharpening.
LENS MODULATION OPTIMIZERCorrects lens diffraction and edge softness — best left ON.
COLOR SPACEChoose the color space — sRGB for web/sharing (recommended while traveling), Adobe RGB for professional printing.
HDR MODEReduces lost detail in highlights and shadows at once, similar to D Range Priority.

Not every setting is always available — it depends on how the image was shot.

Exercise: practice RAW conversion SOS: shots suddenly black & white

Manuals

PDFOfficial manualDownload, 7 MB — with every illustration, works offline (ships with the app) WEBOnline manual, X-H2SSearchable, phone-friendly (Fujifilm's website)

The manual text lives on the “Manual” page (search works offline); the PDF with illustrations is available there via the download button.
